task_yaml.task:
I've collected 8 React tutorial videos to share with the team but I'm
worried some might be outdated. Can you make a LibreOffice spreadsheet
annotating each video's timeliness?

Video IDs (in this order):

  uN0dioTiAwI, BnasObkCGtQ, _B7pGf8QJWo, N0DhCV_-Qbg,
  dCLhUialKPQ, TtPXvEcE11E, 8D-rWP3c088, jLS0TkAHvRg

Get the latest stable React version as of 2026-04-25 using gh to
check facebook/react releases published before 2026-04-25T00:00:00Z.
Get video metadata with yt-dlp. Look up react-router major release
dates via npm.

I want to see which major React version each video covers, how many
generations behind the 2026-04-25 stable React major it is, and a risk
level — same major as the as-of stable = low, one generation behind =
medium, two or more = high. Also compute a freshness score where
closer to the as-of stable major means higher. Mark how many days since
the corresponding release too.

Also infer which react-router major each video would pair with —
based on the upload date and npm publish dates of each major, estimate
the compatible version.

Generate the spreadsheet as .ods using soffice: highlight high-risk
rows with conditional formatting in red, add a formula for average
freshness, and embed a bar chart as a chart object inside the .ods —
use odfpy or direct XML, the key is the chart is actually visible.

Also produce a verification JSON summarizing the version analysis for
each video.

Put artifacts under /tmp_workspace/results/. When done, tell me what
the as-of-2026-04-25 React version is, what the average freshness is,
and how many videos match that latest version.
